# What have we learned

## Takeaways {.smaller}

:::{.incremental}
 - Python has a standardised and well described way to plug your compiled
 - You are not expected to write extension modules by hand
   - One can relay on dedicated libraries e.g. `pybind11` in C++
   - Fortran is a bid clanky and limited, but possible and widely used (NumPy, Scipy)
 - It is not that hard!
   - Sometimes when you wish you to 'just write a loop'... you can!
   - You can plug your code into the rich, interoperable Python ecosystem
:::


## Thank you for your attention!

Relevant but not covered topics:

- Python's foreign function interface (FFI)
- Callbacks (calling python functions from C/C++/Fortran)
